headings.text: Contravention Code 01
Parked in a restricted street during prescribed hours. You were parked on a yellow line (single or double) or in a street where there are temporary waiting restrictions
How to have your penalty charge notice (PCN) for this parking contravention cancelled.
Follow our 3 stage process:
Specific grounds of appeal for this contravention
Signs and lines
Controlled parking zones
Below we show diagrams of the signs and lines which must be in situ by law and much more information that will help you decide whether to appeal against your penalty charge notice.
Lines
Loading

sections.heading: Specific grounds of appeal for this contravention
sections.contentPreview: Go to top

sections.heading: Signs and lines
sections.contentPreview: This section is very important. We can’t emphasise enough how often the signage is incorrect or indeed there is no signage whatsoever. Road markings can conflict with the signs and quite often the road markings are not clearly visible. If the signs or lines are wrong then you will have grounds to ha

sections.heading: Controlled parking zones
sections.contentPreview: You can obtain maps showing the borders of a controlled parking zone from the enforcing authority and often from their websites. A Controlled Parking Zone (CPZ) is a designated area (usually a town centre) which has the same enforcement hours on single yellow lines and normally chargeable hours on p

sections.heading: Below we show diagrams of the signs and lines which must be in situ by law and much more information that will help you decide whether to appeal against your penalty charge notice.
Lines
sections.contentPreview: Road Markings must be present, clear, unobstructed by leaves etc and of the correct width in order for any restrictions to be enforceable. Please note the information regarding sizes and the fact that no variants are permitted.     Go to top

sections.heading: Loading
sections.contentPreview: You can park on a single yellow line if you are loading and if loading is not prohibited. (please see the contravention code 2 loading section) Some local authorities allow a grace period on a single yellow line. You should check with them to make sure that you were allowed the grace period. Please

headings.text: Contravention Code 02
Parked or loading/unloading in a restricted street where waiting and loading/unloading restrictions are in force, parked where there is a yellow line AND yellow markings on the kerb. (Blips)
How to have your penalty charge notice (PCN) for this parking contravention cancelled.
Follow our 3 stage process:
Specific grounds of appeal for this contravention
Possible grounds of appeal for this contravention
Signs
Lines

sections.heading: Possible grounds of appeal for this contravention
sections.contentPreview: 1. Loading restrictions are shown by yellow blips on the kerb and there should also be a plate within 15 metres of where you stopped stating the time when loading restrictions apply. One yellow blip on the kerb means no loading or unloading at the times shown on the sign two yellow blips mean No loa

sections.heading: Signs
sections.contentPreview: Below we show the signs and blips on the kerb which must be in situ in order for this contravention to be enforceable. It is important to note the information in the boxes immediately underneath each picture as this shows the circumstance in which the sign can be used and also the permitted variatio

sections.heading: Lines
sections.contentPreview: Road Markings must be present, clear, unobstructed by leaves etc and of the correct width in order for any restrictions to be enforceable. Please note the information regarding sizes and the fact that no variants are permitted.   Adjudicator’s decisions. There have been many adjudicators’ decisions

headings.text: Contravention Code 04
Parked in a meter bay when penalty time is indicated. You must move your car before the time purchased has expired.
How to have your penalty charge notice (PCN) for this parking contravention cancelled.

Specific grounds of appeal for this contravention

sections.heading: Specific grounds of appeal for this contravention
sections.contentPreview: Go to top  1. It is important to note that this contravention code only applies to meter bays and not pay and display bays. If you were parked in a pay and display bay and have been issued with a ticket showing this contravention code then you should appeal and visa versa. 2. Was the ticket issued b

headings.text: Contravention Code 54
Failing to comply with a restriction on vehicles entering and waiting in a pedestrian zone. Contravention code 54
Sample signs:
How to have your penalty charge notice (PCN) for this parking contravention cancelled.
Follow our 3 stage process:
Specific grounds of appeal for this contravention
There are 5 more ways that you can have your penalty charge notice cancelled for this offence. We have even summarised adjudicator’s decisions where motorists have won.

sections.heading: Failing to comply with a restriction on vehicles entering and waiting in a pedestrian zone. Contravention code 54
sections.contentPreview: Go to top  Local authorities are now enforcing this contravention using CCTV enforcement. Quite often however the signs are not sufficiently clear. headings.text: Successes stories of parking ticket appeals
A few of our successes

sections.heading: A few of our successes
sections.contentPreview: Some of our successes include assisting motorists when we pointed out to the London Evening Standard that Westminster council were issuing tickets on New Year’s Day a few years ago to thousands of motorists. Westminster said that New Year’s Day was not a public holiday because it happened to be a Sa
